steps.title: Identify the language-geography alignment
steps.content: The passage directly connects languages to regions: Mandarin is spoken in north China and is dominant in Flushing; Cantonese and Fuzhounese are spoken in south China and are dominant in Chinatown. This consistent correspondence between language and neighborhood is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the settlement inference
steps.content:
Choice A directly fits this alignment: the correspondence logically suggests that people from north China settled where Mandarin is dominant (Flushing) and people from south China settled where Cantonese and Fuzhounese are dominant (Chinatown).

The other choices describe unsupported behaviors, make unmentioned temporal claims, or require population data not provided.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
